symptoms.symptom_name: Deep perineal aching pressure ('sitting on a golf ball'), worse on sitting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sit-worse perineal pressure pain is characteristic of pelvic-floor/prostatic pain syndromes.

symptoms.symptom_name: Post-exertional flare following physical exercise, on a chronic relapsing background

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A recognised post-exertional trigger with prior similar flares supports a chronic pelvic pain pattern rather than a new acute pathology.

symptoms.symptom_name: Irritative voiding symptoms — dysuria and urinary frequency

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Irritative LUTS are common in CP/CPPS.
Dysuria also occurs in bacterial infection, so it does not distinguish the two — urinalysis/MSU is required.

symptoms.symptom_name: Anxious, unable to sit still, pacing due to discomfort

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Restlessness reflects pain severity and anxiety; non-specific for diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Still able to pass small amounts of urine — not in complete retention

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved voiding argues against acute retention, though objective bladder assessment is needed to be certain.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, rigors, or systemic toxicity

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever/rigors lowers the probability of bacterial prostatitis/urosepsis but does not exclude it without urinalysis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No sudden severe testicular pain or swelling; gradual perineal onset

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Gradual perineal onset without acute testicular pain/swelling argues strongly against torsion; definitive exclusion needs examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: No urethral discharge and no visible haematuria

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of discharge and haematuria lowers the probability of urethritis/STI, though a urine test is still appropriate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal-sized, boggy, exquisitely tender prostate without nodules; normal non-tender testes/epididymis; non-palpable/non-percussible bladder (in-person examination findings)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A boggy, tender, non-nodular prostate with normal testes and an empty bladder supports CP/CPPS and excludes torsion and gross retention. Clinician-only findings sealed from the AI-Doctor-readable layer.
Normal non-tender testes on examination argue strongly against torsion; cannot be assessed via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Urinalysis dipstick negative for nitrites and leukocytes (in-person investigation finding)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A dipstick negative for nitrites/leukocytes lowers the probability of bacterial infection, supporting a non-bacterial flare; not obtainable via telehealth and does not replace MSU culture.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Preserved voiding, no systemic sepsis features, and no acute testicular pain/swelling together constitute a negative urological-emergency screen supporting a non-emergency pathway, provided in-person confirmation and safety-netting follow.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person genitourinary examination (prostate, testes) and bladder assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Needed to confirm CP/CPPS and exclude torsion/retention that telehealth cannot assess.
Establishes or excludes a bacterial component before any antibiotic decision; cannot be obtained via chat.
Antibiotics are not indicated for non-bacterial CP/CPPS and should not be started blindly; drives antimicrobial resistance without benefit.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Scrotal ultrasound if any suspicion of torsion
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Emergency imaging only if sudden testicular pain/swelling emerges; not indicated for the current perineal flare with normal testes.
